description_long: This is a three-story brick house with a symmetrical facade and a steep, dark hip roof. The front features large, evenly spaced rectangular windows and a central double-door entryway flanked by small potted plants. On one side, there are two balconies with blue railings, while the opposite side has a single balcony and a garage door. Mature trees and shrubs are positioned at the corners, adding greenery to the paved surroundings. The windows are arranged in neat horizontal and vertical rows, giving the building a formal, orderly appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century urban residential architecture, blending traditional brickwork with modern balcony elements.
description: A three-story brick house with a hip roof, symmetrical windows, balconies, and a central entryway, surrounded by trees and paved ground.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entryway with double doors, symmetrical windows, and potted plants flanking the entrance.
description_view_1: This side view highlights two blue-railed balconies and a large tree partially obscuring the facade.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a garage door, a single balcony, and a more utilitarian arrangement of windows.
description_view_3: The opposite side view features the main entryway canopy, a tree, and a more shaded, landscaped area.
attribute_entryway_design: Central double-door entry with a canopy, flanked by potted plants and accessed from a paved area.
